digest.top_line: NRG/RTOG 0848: adjuvant chemoRT null for OS overall (HR 0.96, p=0.38); only signal is an exploratory node-negative subgroup (5yr OS 48.1% vs 28.6%).
digest.tldr: Adjuvant chemoRT after resected pancreatic-head cancer gives no overall OS benefit, reinforcing chemo-alone practice (ESPAC-1, CONKO-001). The lone positive read is an exploratory node-negative subgroup (5yr OS 48.1% vs 28.6%, P-interaction 0.022), hypothesis-generating and gemcitabine-era. Not a basis to add RT.

sites.intro: Adjuvant pancreatic chemoRT stays off the table; RTOG 0848's only positive read is an exploratory node-negative subgroup that predates the mFOLFIRINOX era.

studies.name: NRG Oncology/RTOG 0848
studies.tldr: No OS benefit from adjuvant CXRT overall (HR 0.96, p=0.38); node-negative subgroup 5yr OS 48.1% vs 28.6% favoring CXRT.

details.text: 📊 1° EP OS null overall: HR 0.96 (90% CI 0.79-1.18), p=0.38 (1-sided log-rank)
📐 Median OS 2.3 vs 2.6 yr (Chemo+CXRT vs Chemo); adding RT numerically favors chemo alone
📊 5yr OS estimate by nodal status (2-sided 95% CI)
🔍 Phase III RT randomization; N=354, resected pancreatic head adenoca after gemcitabine-based adjuvant chemo, ± fluoropyrimidine-sensitized CXRT

details: 🔍 RT dose, fractionation, and target volume not reported in source text

details.text: ⚠️ Node-negative OS benefit is an exploratory subgroup (nodal P-interaction 0.022); hypothesis-generating, needs independent confirmation
⚠️ Gemcitabine-era backbone (gem alone 236, gem+erlotinib 100); predates adjuvant mFOLFIRINOX standard

details: 🔗 Reinforces adjuvant chemo-alone practice (ESPAC-1, CONKO-001); no routine role for adjuvant chemoRT

verdict.rationale: Prespecified phase III primary OS null (HR 0.96); reinforces adjuvant chemo-alone standard. Node-negative benefit is exploratory subgroup, not practice-defining.
verdict.audience: Resected pancreatic head adenocarcinoma, post gemcitabine-based adjuvant chemo

studies.significance: The RT read lives in the node-negative subgroup: 5yr OS 48.1% vs 28.6% and median OS 3.9 vs 3.0 yr favoring CXRT (nodal P-interaction 0.022), against a null overall HR 0.96. Dose, fractionation, and target volume are not in source, so transferability is unverified. It reopens selective CXRT in node-negative resected disease, not routine addition.
studies.monday_clinic: In resected node-negative pancreatic head adenocarcinoma after gemcitabine-based adjuvant chemo, this reopens fluoropyrimidine CXRT as a hypothesis to test; it does not extend to node-positive or unselected resected pts, who gained no OS benefit.

analysis_sections.label: Design
analysis_sections.body: Radiotherapy randomization of NRG/RTOG 0848. **Phase 3** RCT: adjuvant chemotherapy **± fluoropyrimidine-sensitized chemoradiotherapy** after gemcitabine-based chemo. **N=354** (Chemo 174 vs Chemo+CXRT 180).

analysis_sections.label: Population & inclusion
analysis_sections.body: Curative-intent resected **pancreatic head** adenocarcinoma, completing gemcitabine-based adjuvant chemo without progression. **Node-positive predominated** (N1 263 vs N0 91); T3 288 vs T1/T2 66.

analysis_sections.label: Regimen
analysis_sections.body: Adjuvant systemic mostly **gemcitabine alone (236)** or **gemcitabine+erlotinib (100)**; non-oxaliplatin gemcitabine combinations 18. Experimental arm added fluoropyrimidine-sensitized RT.

analysis_sections.label: Radiotherapy
analysis_sections.body: Fluoropyrimidine-sensitized chemoradiotherapy. **Dose, fractionation, and target volume not reported in source text**, limiting transfer to practice.

analysis_sections.label: Endpoints
analysis_sections.body: **Primary: overall survival** (all randomized). Also DFS and prespecified subgroup analyses including **pathologic nodal status**.

analysis_sections.label: Results
analysis_sections.body: Overall OS null: **HR 0.96** (90% CI 0.79-1.18), p=0.38; median 2.3 vs 2.6 yr. Node-negative subgroup favored CXRT (**P-interaction 0.022**); see subgroup tables.

analysis_sections.label: vs leading data
analysis_sections.body: Consistent with the shift to adjuvant **chemotherapy alone** (ESPAC-1, CONKO-001); **no routine role** for adjuvant chemoRT. Gemcitabine-era backbone predates adjuvant mFOLFIRINOX (PRODIGE-24).

analysis_sections.label: Applies to
analysis_sections.body: Represents resected pancreatic head adenocarcinoma completing gemcitabine-based adjuvant chemo without progression. Does not represent borderline/unresectable disease, mFOLFIRINOX-treated pts, or node-negative disease as a proven RT indication.

analysis_sections.label: Limitations
analysis_sections.body: **Node-negative benefit is exploratory** (P-interaction 0.022), not the primary result. Gemcitabine-era systemic backbone. RT dose, technique, and follow-up duration not reported in source.

open_questions: Does adjuvant chemoRT benefit node-negative resected pancreatic cancer
Role of adjuvant chemoRT with a modern FOLFIRINOX backbone

related_trials.brief_title: Adjuvant Chemoradiotherapy Versus Chemotherapy for Pancreatic Cancer (ADJUPANC)
related_trials.brief_summary: In this trial, we aim to compare the outcomes of adjuvant chemoradiotherapy with chemotherapy for patients with resected pancreatic cancer who are at high risk of disease progressions.
related_trials.eligibility_brief:

related_trials.brief_title: Radiotherapy With GX Regimen as Adjuvant Therapy for High-risk Patients Following Pancreatic Cancer Surgery
related_trials.brief_summary: Pancreatic cancer is a highly fatal malignant tumor. Simple surgical operations can no longer meet the treatment needs of pancreatic cancer patients. Postoperative adjuvant chemotherapy has a significant effect, which can effectively prevent or delay tumor recurrence and prolong the overall survival period of pancreatic cancer patients. papers.title: Adjuvant Chemotherapy +/- Chemoradiotherapy for Adenocarcinoma of The Pancreatic Head: Results of The Radiotherapy Randomization of NRG Oncology/RTOG 0848
